misclassified.why: Pure static input data (a mitochondrial-dynamics paragraph) consumed by knowledge-workflow tests; contains no test logic or assertions. The enum distinguishes 'asset' (static resource) from 'test' (test code). Verified tests/fixtures/dummy_paper.txt:1-2 is prose data only. Borderline since it lives under tests/, but it is a fixture resource, not a test.

Static fixture: a 7-line sample_function used as input to engineering-workflow tests (tests/fixtures/dummy_code.py:1-7). No test functions/imports/assertions — it is input data that happens to be .py. Defensibly 'asset' rather than 'test' under the asset-vs-test distinction; flagged as borderline.

missed_entry_points: tests/test_kg_direct.py:78 — standalone executable script (shebang #!/usr/bin/env python3 at line 1, sys.path.insert at line 5) whose `if __name__ == "__main__": test_kg_extraction()` block runs extract_kg_triples directly via `python tests/test_kg_direct.py` and writes side-effect file kg_extraction_results.txt; a genuine runnable entry point absent from the Stage-1 six-entry-point enumeration.

tests/test_full_workflow.py:59 — `if __name__ == "__main__": test_full_workflow()` block (shebang at line 1) intended as a directly-runnable script invoking run_knowledge_flow; NOTE it calls test_full_workflow() without the required pytest `tmp_path` arg (line 7), so direct execution raises TypeError — a non-functional/broken entry point, but still an unlisted __main__ invocation surface.

promptverge/main.py:132 — `if __name__ == "__main__": app()` makes main.py directly runnable (`python promptverge/main.py`) in addition to the console-script and `python -m promptverge` paths; same Typer app, but a distinct invocation surface not separately enumerated.
